summary: Day 2 of the downtrend, and it broke wider. Equities extended Monday's TSMOM flip hard as an Asian chip crash (KOSPI -9.99%, Samsung and SK Hynix down more than 12%), a Broadcom guidance miss, and Fed late-2026 hike bets hammered the AI trade: MNQ closed 29,750 (-2.9%) below a falling 30,031 short stop and MES 7,446 below 7,482. The lone haven long broke too, with gold flipping short (4,129 below its 4,178.6 long stop), so all four HTA instruments are now short and even safety was sold. TSMOM-20 (long-only) stays flat and stands aside until a confirmed reclaim above 30,031.

setups.name: AVWAP Hand-off (SHORT)
setups.entry: On a rally / hand-off into NYVWAP 29,770 or the 30,031 ATR stop that rejects on an hourly close, short the continuation
setups.stop: A confirmed hourly close back above 30,031
setups.bias: Today was its habitat: downside OR break that held, sell rallies into VWAP. Elevated volatility means wider stops and smaller size. Not eligible Wednesday (gate).

setups.name: TSMOM-20 long re-arm watch
setups.entry: No long while price is below the 30,031 falling stop. Re-arm only on a confirmed hourly close back above 30,031
setups.bias: Long-only and stopped out Monday. Flat is the position. Do not bottom-fish a long under a falling stop; wait for the reclaim.

event.name: No scheduled first-tier US release (Asian semis crash drove the tape)
event.consensus: After Monday's TSMOM flip, the question was whether the unwind was a one-day shakeout or the start of a real downtrend.
event.actual: It extended. An Asian chip-sector crash (KOSPI -9.99%, Samsung and SK Hynix down more than 12% on overheating-regulation signals), a Broadcom guidance disappointment, and rising Fed late-2026 hike bets halted the AI trade and broadened the sell-off.
event.surprise: Downtrend day 2. Monday's flip was confirmed and extended; even gold was sold, a sign of broad liquidation rather than rotation.
event.nqReaction: MNQ fell 2.9% to 29,750, below a falling 30,031 short stop. ATR(14) expanded to 157 (from 117). TSMOM-20 long stays flat with no long habitat.
event.crossAsset: MES 7,446 below 7,482 (ES confirms). MGC flipped short, 4,129 below its 4,178.6 long stop, the lone haven long breaking. MCL ground lower to 73.04, short intact. VIX +11% to 19.19. Cash and the dollar were the only haven.

observe.name: Gold haven break
observe.note: Gold flipping short with equities removes the last intact trend. Watch whether this is liquidation-driven (snaps back once forced selling clears) or a genuine change in the haven bid.

observe.name: Volatility expansion
observe.note: ATR(14) and VIX both expanding into a two-day decline. Mean-reversion setups stay suppressed until volatility stabilizes; trend structure dominates.

risk.throttleState: DEFENSIVE. All four instruments are below their ATR stops; the long-only stack is flat with no long habitat.
risk.ddProximity: Contained. TSMOM-20 exited on Monday's flip and added no new risk; flat through the day-2 extension.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Full budget intact. The long-only system took no trades today; flat is the position.
risk.sizing: NQ, ES, gold and crude are all below their ATR stops, so the long habitat is OFF and long-only takes no shorts: flat across the board. Gold's haven long, the one trend that held Monday, broke today.
risk.note: Volatility is expanding into a two-day liquidation; the binding constraint is the urge to bottom-fish a long-only long into falling knives. Stay DEFENSIVE until a confirmed reclaim re-arms the long. Week ahead: final Q1 GDP and jobless claims Thursday Jun 25; May PCE inflation Friday Jun 26 is the catalyst.

psychology: Monday the flip was the news; today the temptation is to call a bottom. A two-day, volatility-expanding decline where even gold gets sold is liquidation, and liquidations rarely bottom on the first green hourly bar. For a long-only system the discipline is patience: flat is a full position, and the re-arm trigger is mechanical (a confirmed hourly close back above 30,031), not a feeling that it has fallen enough. Let price reclaim the stop before you reclaim the long. Chasing strength you did not plan for is how a clean defensive day turns into a bad offensive one.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 22 map, checked on the Jun 23 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultMeasured
1MNQ reclaims 30,863 (flipped stop) on an hourly close (flip negates)NOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. No hourly close reclaimed 30,863; the highest globex close was ~30,602. The downtrend flip stood.
2MNQ stays below 30,863 and loses 30,503 RTH low on an hourly close (downtrend extends)TRIGGERED - PLAYED OUTOvernight closes broke 30,503 (30,461, 30,405, 30,241, 30,108) and the trend extended to 29,577. Rallies into AVWAP/stop were supply, exactly as scripted.
3MES holds below its 7,582 stop (risk-off persists, aligned short)TRIGGERED - PLAYED OUTMES held below 7,582 all session (high 7,491), lost 7,513 (RTH low 7,429) and closed lower at 7,446. ES confirmed NQ.
4MGC holds above its 4,179 rising stop on hourly closes (haven long confirmed)TRIGGERED - FAILEDGold did NOT hold 4,179. It lost the stop on overnight hourly closes and flipped short, closing 4,129. The bias's own warning (losing 4,179 is the first crack) is what played out.
5MCL stays below 75.18 / 75 psych (crude downtrend intact)TRIGGERED - PLAYED OUTCrude stayed below 75.18 all session (high 74.50), rallies into 74+ were sold, closed 73.04. Downtrend intact.

fullReport.html:
Map for Wednesday, June 24, 2026. Filters: Wednesday gate blocks HOF and MID; BIR and BNC eligible. No FOMC/CPI. Calendar: no first-tier US release Wednesday; final Q1 GDP and jobless claims Thursday Jun 25, May PCE inflation Friday Jun 26 is the week catalyst. All levels from today's 1hr chart.

IFTHENBIAS
MNQ rallies into 29,770 AVWAP / 30,031 ATR stop and rejects on an hourly closeDowntrend intact, sell-the-rip continuesFailed retests of 29,770 then 30,031 are the short trigger; only a confirmed hourly close back above 30,031 negates the downtrend

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Day 2 of the downtrend played the script. Rows 2, 3, 5 triggered and PLAYED OUT (NQ extended, ES confirmed, crude ground lower). Row 4 (gold) triggered and FAILED its bullish thesis: the haven long broke and flipped short, exactly the crack the bias warned about. Row 1 not triggered (no 30,863 reclaim).
